prompt: Write an Adams CMD script that creates a chain of 8 point masses in a straight line along the X axis, spaced 50 mm apart, starting at the origin. The first mass is fixed to ground with a fixed joint. Each adjacent pair of masses is connected by a spring-damper with stiffness 100 N/mm, damping coefficient 1.0 N·s/mm, and free length 50 mm. Each mass is 0.25 kg. Use a FOR loop with parametric string construction (EVAL, RTOI, //) to create the masses and spring-dampers rather than writing them out individually. Simulate for 2 seconds.

assertions.text: A for / end loop drives mass and/or spring-damper creation
EVAL() used to dereference a variable inside a command string
RTOI() used to convert the loop counter to a string for name construction
// string concatenation operator used to build part/marker names
Masses created with part create point_mass (not rigid_body)
Spring-dampers created with force create element_like translational_spring_damper with stiffness=100, damping=1.0, displacement_at_preload=50
First mass is fixed to ground with a spherical joint or atpoint primitive (NOT fixed joint — Adams rejects fixed joints on point masses)
Masses are spaced 50 mm apart along the X axis
Script uses simulation single_run transient command
All object names use full dot-path hierarchy
No adams_id specified anywhere in the script
At least one geometry shape (sphere or cylinder) is added to each mass
CMD script runs in Adams View without any ERROR: lines in aview.log
